items.headline: Does Cummins’ Data Center And Trucking Push Reshape The Bull Case For CMI?
items.summary: Cummins recently participated in a Morgan Stanley data center power deep-dive and presented at the Wells Fargo Industrials & Materials Conference, while CEO Jennifer Rumsey joined 3M’s Board and an analyst upgrade highlighted stronger prospects in power generation and North American trucking. This cluster of events underscores how data center power demand and emissions-driven fleet upgrades are becoming increasingly important to Cummins’ long-term business mix. items.headline: Atkore, Cummins, and MasTec Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
items.summary: A number of stocks fell in the afternoon session after the CPI report showed 4.2% annual inflation, the highest in three years, with markets fully pricing a December Fed rate hike.

items.headline: There’s a New Stock to Play the AI Power Boom, and It’s Down Today
items.summary: Newly-listed power company ERock could appeal to investors looking for another way to play the AI boom.  Erock started trading on the New York Stock Exchange around midday on Wednesday, slipping 5% in early action.  ERock makes natural gas-powered engines and generators that can be placed on-site at data centers or other large businesses to provide electricity.

items.headline: 3 Reasons to Avoid CMI and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
items.summary: Since June 2021, the S&P 500 has delivered a total return of 74.5%. But one standout stock has more than doubled the market - over the past five years, Cummins has surged 165% to $668.50 per share. Its momentum hasn’t stopped as it’s also gained 27.4% in the last six months, beating the S&P by 19.9%.
